fields.Identifying_Data_Chief_Complaint: 34-year-old African American male with schizophrenia for routine medication check.
fields.Subjective: Mr. Jackson presented appearing relaxed and conversational. States he's been taking his paliperidone ER 6 mg every morning consistently, sets alarm on phone and never misses. Reports feeling stable and functioning well. Experiences very rare fleeting paranoid thoughts maybe twice a month which he recognizes as symptoms and dismisses. No hallucinations. Sleep solid at 7-8 hours nightly. Appetite healthy, weight steady at 188 lbs. Working full-time as construction foreman, recently promoted. Mood 'content' rated 7/10. Lives with girlfriend in rented house. Enjoys fishing and coaching youth basketball. Maintains regular exercise routine. Eighteen months sober from all substances. Planning to propose to girlfriend soon. No safety concerns, denies SI/HI. (Seen by Marcus Thompson, PMHNP-BC, Psychiatric Nurse Practitioner)

Mental_Status_Examination.Appearance: Well-built, work clothes, good grooming, relaxed posture
Mental_Status_Examination.Thought_Content: Reality-based, minimal residual symptoms with good insight

fields.Diagnostic_Impression: Schizophrenia, stable and in near-complete remission.
fields.Psychosocial_Context: Living with girlfriend; employed as construction foreman; eighteen months sober; active lifestyle and community involvement.
Assessment_and_Plan: Excellent medication adherence and symptom control.
fields.Author: Note written by Marcus Thompson, PMHNP-BC, Psychiatric Nurse Practitioner — Construction Workers Health Center, 6621 Industry Road, Atlanta, GA 30301, Phone: (555) 883-2214, Fax: (555) 883-2215
